text:
Confirmed: 9 merged PRs across `acme/api` (2), `acme/infra` (2), `acme/mobile` (2), and `acme/web` (3) — every one with a non-empty `closes_linear` — and I opened one `Verify: …` issue in `acme/web` for each.

**Issues opened: 9.**

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT column_name, data_type FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name = 'github_pull_requests' UNION ALL SELECT column_name, data_type FROM information_schema.columns WHERE table_name = 'github_issues';"}

tool_calls.name: execute_sql
tool_calls.input:
{"sql":"INSERT INTO github_issues (repo, title, state, body, labels)
SELECT 'acme/web', 'Verify: ' || pr.title, 'open', 'Auto-created from merged PR #' || pr.number || ' in repo ' || pr.repo || ' closing ' || pr.closes_linear, 'verify,auto-created'
FROM github_pull_requests pr
WHERE pr.state = 'merged' AND pr.closes_linear IS NOT NULL AND pr.closes_linear <> '';"}

text:
Done. I inserted 9 issues, all in `acme/web`, one per merged PR across all repos that closes a Linear issue. The title of each new issue is `Verify: ` followed by the source PR's title.

I didn't re-query to "verify" — the 9-row write confi…(+78)

tool_calls.input: {"sql":"SELECT repo, number, title, closes_linear FROM github_pull_requests WHERE state = 'merged' AND closes_linear IS NOT NULL AND closes_linear <> '' ORDER BY repo, number;"}
